mentions.note: Commercial airline parts supplier up 27% YTD; part of great aerospace boom with no sign of slowing; CEO John Holmes recently on show; can't-miss opportunity.
Big four semiconductor capital equipment player; up 29-39% since January; benefits from Taiwan Semi guidance of 52-56B capex (vs. <45B expected) and sustained industry investment cycle.
Semiconductor capital equipment leader best positioned for leading-edge AI chip manufacturing; up 29-39% since January; benefits from Taiwan Semi capex surge and Micron multi-fab expansion.
Took charge on tanker contract per Pentagon efficiency demands; stock would be up big without charge but big position for travel trust; management holding line on dividends.
Down 40% last year, up 5% YTD, trading 34x earnings at four-and-half-year low; warming up to stock with franchise at reduced price; reports Feb 3rd, could retrace to $30 low to buy more.
Optical semiconductor play; Cramer suggests wait for initial price action, then buy if stock gets hit; see how it trades tomorrow.
Optical networking play; many competitors in space; wait for earnings report, buy if stock gets hit.
Won $5.6B 10-year Army contract for AI-powered procurement software; out-of-nowhere win shows Pentagon turning to commercial software solutions; fell 87 cents despite win due to SaaS sector weakness.
Health insurance business tumbled 14% on Medicare Advantage rate freeze; insurance division facing structural margin pressure.
Design software down 20%; Cramer avoids due to Adobe weakness; design software too hard to own with competitive threats; pass.
$30B company with triple-digit growth last year; supplies HVAC for data center and AI stack buildout; Cramer should have kept recommending it lower; buy on dips as great company.
Volatility matching 2011 peak (preceded 50% correction); RSI in mid-90s; broken multi-decade trendline at 2900; hit 5100 yesterday; technical setup suggests potential trend reversal or blowoff.

Won $6B Meta fiber optic cable contract; rallied 15% on news; CEO Weeks says hyperscalers will be biggest customers; fiber superior to copper for data center linking; upside from other hyperscalers and potential future chip applications.
Stock jumped 8.75% on huge profit boost and best full-year market share in decade; beneficiary of relaxed environmental regs and tariff protection; CEO Barra confident 2026 will be even better.
Medicare Advantage rate disappointment crushed stock 21%; lucrative program losing luster under government efficiency mandate.
Reported good numbers but dismal guidance on CPU supply constraints; lacks manufacturing equipment; doesn't have ability to meet chip demand; signals more business for capital equipment providers.
Semiconductor capital equipment maker up 29-39% since January; positioned for consistent orders as Micron, Taiwan Semi, Intel all expanding production capacity through multi-year cycle.

Cramer's personal favorite semiconductor capital equipment company; up 137% last year + 39% January; makes equipment for CPU and memory chips; beat-and-raise baked in but 2027 numbers likely better; buy any dip.
Infrastructure/pipe play up straight since bankruptcy emergence; Cramer strong buyer despite insider selling flag; 'slow and steady wins the race'.
Straight up since bankruptcy; slow-and-steady pipes and infrastructure play; insider selling notwithstanding, Cramer remains buyer of this quality company.
Cancer vaccination promise long-awaited; stock up 54% YTD; Cramer wants to wait and see how certain clinical data is before buying despite good intentions.

CEO Sanjay Mehrotra announced $100B foundry in New York (2030), new fab Idaho (H2 2027), new fab Singapore (H2 2028); multi-year production expansion guarantees sustained capex orders for equipment makers.
Mixed quarter but stock at 52-week high; protected by steel tariffs blocking foreign dumping; CEO Topalian credits very supportive pro-manufacturing trade environment.

Up only 1% YTD despite strength; should catch up with Mag 7 peers; CEO Jensen Wong in China during lunar new year exploring meaningful Chinese business; 25% of China sales going to U.S. government per deal.

$30M in bookings announced January 2026: $10M Fortune 100 quantum compute service deal, $20M FAU quantum system sale; DoD partnership with Deloitte/Andel; moving HQ to Boca Raton; commercial agreements legitimate.
Metallurgical coal play up 34% from December entry; contrarian bet under current administration's pro-domestic manufacturing stance; Cramer sees this as the one where it finally works.
Metallurgical coal up 34% from Dec entry; Cramer recommends staying in as 'great contrary play' under current administration; coal should have its day in sun.
Presidential social media pressure on buyback practices worked; company halted share repurchases to accelerate production and capacity; stock rallied nicely; dividend safe; clear message received.

Up 10-fold from 2020 lows; 57% gain since January; RSI at 94 (vs. 86 in 2011); Bollinger Bands show 3.5 std deviation move (only matched 1979 Hunt corner); resistance 110-120/oz; 19-40 range possible if 2011 pattern repeats.
Propane too wild and inconsistent per Cramer; not worth owning despite dividend profile; too volatile for core holding.
Blowout quarter; guiding 52-56B capex for 2026 (up 27-37%) vs. <45B expected; management says supply-demand won't balance until 2028-2029; bullish for years of equipment orders.
Medicare Advantage rates increased only 0.09% instead of 4-5% expected; stock fell 19%; harsh blow to profitable segment under Oz administration cost-cutting pressure.
